# LYCORE

> LYCORE answers your phones 24/7, builds websites that convert callers, and handles the follow-up — so small service businesses stop losing jobs to missed calls. LYCORE also optimizes your Google Business Profile, automates intake and follow-up paperwork, and is building full operating systems for service industries.

LYCORE (LYCORE GROUP LLC) is based in Albuquerque, NM and works with service businesses across the United States. Contact: services@lycore.org. LYCORE's receptionist service is staffed by a trained system that answers calls, captures caller details, and routes information to your team. It does not replace licensed professionals or make decisions on your behalf. Rankings, revenue, call volume and client outcomes are never guaranteed.

## Services

- [What We Build](https://www.lycore.org/what-we-build): LYCORE answers calls 24/7, builds phone-first websites, optimizes Google Business Profiles, and automates intake for small service businesses that cannot afford to miss a call.

## Company

- [Vision](https://www.lycore.org/vision): LYCORE is building toward complete operating systems for service industries: from answered calls to automated paperwork to end-to-end business infrastructure.
- [Bail Bonds](https://www.lycore.org/industries/bail-bonds): LYCORE serves bail bond agencies with 24/7 call answering and phone-first websites. For full bail bond pricing and service details, visit lycore.org.
- [About](https://www.lycore.org/about): Learn how LYCORE GROUP LLC builds customer communication and workflow systems for service businesses that need reliable response and follow-up.
- [FAQ](https://www.lycore.org/faq): Answers to common questions about LYCORE 24/7 call answering, websites, Google Business Profile optimization, pricing, and how it all works for service businesses.
- [Contact](https://www.lycore.org/contact): Get a free review of how your service business handles calls, website visitors, and follow-up. No pressure, no obligation.
- [Book a Call](https://www.lycore.org/book): Schedule a time to discuss how LYCORE can help your service business answer more calls and book more jobs.
- [Commitments](https://www.lycore.org/commitments): How LYCORE approaches its work, people and wider responsibilities as a remote-first, early-stage company.
- [Career Growth](https://www.lycore.org/commitments/career-growth): How LYCORE approaches practical learning, feedback, responsibility and career development as the company grows.
- [Work-Life Balance](https://www.lycore.org/commitments/work-life-balance): The LYCORE remote-first working arrangement, flexibility expectations and work-life balance commitments.
- [Social Impact](https://www.lycore.org/commitments/social-impact): The public LYCORE pledge for responsible technology, fair access, privacy, inclusive communication and practical support.
- [How We Work](https://www.lycore.org/commitments/how-we-work): The principles that guide how LYCORE approaches ownership, communication, learning and client work.

## Frequently Asked Questions

- Q: What kind of businesses does LYCORE work with?
  A: LYCORE works with small service businesses where a missed call means lost revenue: towing companies, plumbers, roofers, HVAC contractors, electricians, restaurants, urgent care clinics, bail bond agencies, locksmiths, restoration companies, and similar businesses.
- Q: How does LYCORE answer my calls?
  A: When a call comes in and your team cannot pick up, our receptionist answers on your behalf. It greets the caller by your business name, captures the details you need (name, location, urgency, service type), and routes the information to your team by text, email, or dashboard. Your callers talk to a trained receptionist, not a voicemail box.
- Q: Is the receptionist a real person or a computer?
  A: The receptionist uses artificial intelligence to hold natural conversations, capture caller details, and route information. It is not a human, but it is trained on your business specifics — your services, your hours, your service area — so callers get accurate, helpful responses instead of a generic voicemail.
- Q: Will callers know they are not talking to my staff?
  A: The receptionist introduces itself as part of your team and answers using your business name. Most callers will not notice a difference. If a caller specifically asks, the receptionist will answer honestly.
- Q: What happens after the call is answered?
  A: Your team receives a summary with the caller name, phone number, service requested, location, and urgency level. You can receive this by text, email, or through your existing tools. Nothing falls through the cracks.
- Q: Can LYCORE transfer live calls to my team?
  A: Yes. When a call needs immediate human attention, the receptionist can transfer the caller directly to your on-call staff. You set the rules for when live transfers happen.
- Q: How are the websites different from what I can build myself?
  A: LYCORE websites are built specifically for service businesses that depend on phone calls. The phone number is tappable from the first screen, pages load in under two seconds on mobile, and every element is arranged to get an urgent searcher to call — not browse. We also connect Google Business Profile, call tracking, and lead routing so you can see which pages are generating calls.
- Q: What does Google Business Profile optimization include?
  A: We make sure your listing has the right categories, service areas, hours, photos, and description so that when someone nearby searches for what you do, your business appears in the local results. We also monitor and update the profile as Google makes changes.
- Q: What is the intake and follow-up automation?
  A: After a call or form submission, the system captures the details, sends confirmations to your caller, routes the lead to your team, and follows up if nobody has responded. At the end of each month, you receive a report showing how many leads came in, how many were handled, and where they came from. This feature is currently rolling out.
- Q: What does LYCORE cost?
  A: Pricing depends on what your business needs. Start with a free review — we will look at how your calls, website, and follow-up are working today and tell you plainly what would help and what it costs. No pressure, no obligation.
- Q: Do I need to sign a long-term contract?
  A: No. LYCORE does not require long-term contracts. We believe the work should speak for itself month over month.
